channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Capitol standoff spills into court as Minnesota Legislature gets off to rocky start
item.description: With accusations flying of which party is ignoring state law, the Minnesota Legislature’s session could be tinged with plenty of visits to the courthouse.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Rubio to pitch foreign policy credentials to Senate as he vies to become America's top diplomat
item.description: Republican Florida Sen. Marco Rubio to face questions from his colleagues on the Senate Foreign Relations Committee at his confirmation hearing for secretary of state Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump attorney general nominee Pam Bondi to testify before Judiciary Committee
item.description: Trump's attorney general pick, Pam Bondi, to face questioning from the Senate Judiciary Committee on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: John Ratcliffe, tapped by Trump to lead the CIA, will face questioning in the Senate
item.description: Lawmakers on Capitol Hill will question President-elect Donald Trump's pick to lead the CIA on his vision for America's premier spy agency.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about Trump's attorney general pick Pam Bondi as she faces questioning on Capitol Hill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump's pick to lead the Justice Department, Pam Bondi, is set to face questions on Capitol Hill on Wednesday over her loyalty to the Republican president-elect, who has vowed to use the agency to pursue revenge on his perceived political enemies.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP Sen. Joni Ernst officially backs Pete Hegseth for DOD after initial uncertainty
item.description: Sen. Joni Ernst revealed she is officially supporting Pete Hegseth for Secretary of Defense after Tuesday's hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Freedom Caucus takes control of the Wyoming House, marking its first chance to lead
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — As President-elect Donald Trump plans bold moves for his first days in office, so too are conservative lawmakers in Wyoming, the first state where Trump-friendly Freedom Caucus members have won control of a statehouse chamber.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ racing the clock to enshrine 'woke' policing rules, lawyer says, as judge hears Breonna Taylor reform case
item.description: A marathon seven-hour Louisville police reform consent decree hearing did not produce the results the Biden DOJ appeared to hope for, according to one attorney.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP tax leaders: US small businesses could pay more tax than small businesses do in China if Trump cuts expire
item.description: Republican members of the chief House committee on taxes said if Trump's tax cuts expire small businesses could begin paying a higher tax rate than small businesses do in communist China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top 5 moments from Pete Hegseth's Senate confirmation hearing
item.description: Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump's choice to head up the Pentagon, faced a Senate confirmation grilling from the Armed Services Committee.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Obama will skip Trump inauguration, but ex-Presidents Obama, Clinton and Bush will be there
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Former first lady Michelle Obama will skip the inauguration of President-elect Donald Trump, the second time in two weeks that she is not attending a gathering of former U.S. leaders and their spouses, but former Presidents Barack Obama, George W.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Michelle Obama's absence was notable given her tough past criticism of Trump, who succeeded her husband in office.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Hochul's tax cut plan: What it means for New Yorkers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Tuesday announced a plan to cut taxes for more than 8.3 million taxpayers in New York. The proposal was announced as part of her 2025 State of the State address.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Biden moves to lift state sponsor of terrorism designation for Cuba, part of deal to free prisoners
item.description: President Joe Biden has notified Congress of his intent to lift the U.S. designation of Cuba as a state sponsor of terrorism, the White House announced, as part of a deal facilitated by the Catholic Church to free political prisoners on the island.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Aamer Madhani, Colleen Long And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump says he will create an 'External Revenue Service' agency to collect tariff income
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday announced plans to create a new agency called the External Revenue Service to collect tariffs and other revenues from foreign nations.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hirono grills Hegseth over allegations of drinking on the job
item.description: Secretary of Defense nominee Pete Hegseth’s confirmation hearing got heated when Sen. Mazie Hirono (D-Hawaii) challenged him over press reports that he drank while on the job in his previous employment, charges that Hegseth denied.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Trump said he will do on Day 1
item.description: Inauguration Day is Jan. 20, an anticipated day for the "Make America Great Again" president, who has vowed to take swift action on various policies on day one.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: California Democrats approve $50M to fight Trump policies
item.description: One lawmaker said they don't have to choose "between helping LA recover from wildfires and protecting CA from Trump.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: Prosecutor who investigated Hunter Biden defends probes, denounces president's remarks in new report
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The criminal charges against Hunter Biden “were the culmination of thorough, impartial investigations, not partisan politics,” the prosecutor who led the probes said in a report that sharply criticized President Joe Biden for having maligned the Justice Department when he pardoned
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats' hopes of derailing Trump nominees fading fast
item.description: Democrats' hopes of defeating any of President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees appear to be fizzling as Senate committees prepare for the first week of hearings.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Special counsel in Hunter Biden case denounces president for criticism of the probe
item.description: In a final report on his investigation, special counsel David Weiss said President Biden's criticism "undermines the very foundation of what makes America's justice system fair and equitable.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis calls special session to implement Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: “I’m going to call the Legislature into special session starting the week of Jan. 27,” DeSantis said at a press conference Monday.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Trump is planning 100 executive orders starting Day 1 on border, deportations and other priorities
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is preparing more than 100 executive orders starting Day One of the new White House, in what amounts to a shock-and-awe campaign on border security, deportations and a rush of other policy priorities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge Cannon paves the way for potential release of part 1 of special counsel’s report
item.description: Cannon denied a motion by President-elect Donald Trump's former co-defendants who sought to block the release of special counsel Jack Smith's report on the election interference case.
item.dc:creator: NPR Washington Desk, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth’s views on women in combat, infidelity and more
item.description: As author of several books and a former Fox News Channel host, Hegseth has been forced to defend himself against a long record of his own public comments, including in his most recent book, “The War on Warriors: Behind the Betrayal of the Men Who Keep Us Free.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Key Republican says he will support Gabbard as Trump DNI pick
item.description: Sen. James Lankford (R-Okla.) said in a Sunday interview that he will support President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Bessent to divest from assets on road to leading Treasury
item.description: President-elect Trump’s Treasury Secretary pick Scott Bessent has plans to divest from funds and other investments.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: DFL, GOP jockey for power in Minnesota House a day before session’s start
item.description: House Republicans ripped DFLers over tactics that could keep the House from conducting business Tuesday. Melissa Hortman, the House’s top DFLer, defended her moves, telling MPR News that denying a quorum is “the only negotiating power I have right now.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Rahm Emanuel steps down as Japan ambassador ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: “Last year, I spoke at the U.S. Naval War College and found it both rewarding and reaffirming,” Emanuel said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada's Cortez Masto to reintroduce bill to fund fight against wildfires
item.description: In the wake of the devastating California wildfires, Nevada Democratic Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto plans to reintroduce a proposal in Congress to better fund fire departments, interagency coordination and recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mitchell bid to delay burglary trial is latest twist in presession runup for Minnesota lawmakers
item.description: With the Capitol power situation already cloudy, a DFL state senator facing a burglary trial is asking that it be postponed. The tied Senate and closely divided Minnesota could face startup problems next week.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Investigation clears Duluth mayor’s campaign manager and partner
item.description: An external investigation commissioned by the city of Duluth has found Mayor Roger Reinert’s partner and campaign manager Amber Gurske did not act improperly through her communications with city staff early in his administration.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: LA Mayor Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds at airport
item.description: Video taken on an airport jetway shows Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass repeatedly ignoring questions from a reporter about her response to the wildfires ripping through the area this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada Democrats join Republicans in bill to detain migrants accused of certain crimes
item.description: All five members of Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation said they support a Republican proposal to detain immigrants in the country illegally who commit certain crimes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ex-FBI informant who fabricated bribery story about Biden and his son Hunter gets 6 years in prison
item.description: A former FBI informant who fabricated a story about President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes that became central to Republicans’ impeachment effort was sentenced Wednesday to six years in prison.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
